_source: OctoPack (arXiv 2308.07124), Table 15 -- bug-type distribution of the HumanEvalFix Python split.
_note: Counts sum to 164 (the full HumanEvalFix benchmark). These are category COUNTS, not per-problem labels. Populate `problem_bug_type` in Phase 1 from the dataset's own bug-type field, then eval/taxonomy_breakdown.py can produce a per-category pass@1 table for any generations file.
hardest_category_reason: Excess-logic bugs require the model to DELETE code rather than add or change it; OctoPack reports these as the hardest to fix.

missing_logic.description: Correct code is missing (a needed statement/branch/condition was omitted). Fix = add code.

excess_logic.description: Extra, incorrect code is present. Fix = delete code. Empirically the hardest category.

value_misuse.description: A wrong literal/constant/value is used. Fix = change a value.

operator_misuse.description: A wrong operator is used (e.g. < instead of <=, + instead of -). Fix = change an operator.

variable_misuse.description: A wrong variable/identifier is referenced. Fix = change a variable.

function_misuse.description: A wrong function/method is called. Fix = change a call.

problem_bug_type._placeholder: Map each HumanEvalFix task_id -> category key above. Fill in Phase 1 from the dataset.
